reviewer: hand-reviewed (Opus) — desktop+mobile screenshots + conformance runner evidence

rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. Source calls the real token-classification pipeline in a Web Worker (models/bert-ner/worker.js + ner.js). No canned path.
rubric.notes: Real Xenova/bert-base-NER ONNX via transformers.js. Headless did not download the ~110 MB model (correct — download-free run), so live-output semantics are covered by the manual io-shape row.

rubric.evidence: The 'How the API works' section documents the true output shape (per-WordPiece BIO rows: {entity:'B-PER', word, score, index}), and the 'See inside' panel renders per-token tags + confidences. io-shape-semantic=manual (real run needs the model on device).
rubric.notes: Expected shape is correct and legibly explained. Semantic sanity of a real list of entities awaits a driven WASM run on a real device.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id=pass (Xenova/bert-base-NER linked, noted as ONNX build of dslim/bert-base-NER), declares-task=pass (token-classification), declares-backend=pass (WebAssembly — 'no WebGPU needed'), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (worker.js). Desktop screenshot confirms the at-a-glance table.
rubric.notes: Honest, complete runtime disclosure — model id, task, quant, backend, size, license all present and accurate.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot shows the honest 'This model isn't on your device yet.' download-required state on a fresh profile — no auto-download of the ~110 MB model. shared-auto-init-loader=pass (createModelLoader).
rubric.notes: Matches the auto-init contract: absent model => labelled Download, never a silent transfer. auto-init-on-cached (returning-user path) still to be exercised with a warm cache.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass (.err surface). honest-progress-retry-offline + runs-without-webgpu-fallback = manual.
rubric.notes: Loading/ready/error states are wired accessibly; download-progress and offline-cached paths need a driven run to fully evidence.

rubric.evidence: Real controls present: textarea, four sample-text chips, inline highlight output, confidence-on-hover, and a per-token 'see inside' strip. controls-operable=manual (screenshots show controls render; full keyboard/focus pass not yet driven).
rubric.notes: Chips are real button elements and the textarea is standard; a driven keyboard traversal (focus order + visible focus on every control) is the remaining check.

rubric.evidence: Desktop screenshot: the output area shows no entities before a run and the loader shows the honest absent-model state — nothing canned is presented as live output.
conformance: images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Status region uses role=status aria-live; output uses aria-live=polite.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full SR pass over the token-tag 'see inside' strip would be a nice add.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass (scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280x800 and 360x740). Screenshots confirm clean reflow both classes: at-a-glance table stacks, entity legend chips wrap, Georgia display + humanist body legible, no horizontal overflow.
rubric.notes: Layout parity is genuinely good on both classes. parity-both-classes stays manual pending a driven interaction pass on the below-the-fold controls; support kept at needs-review (honest) until then.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a Web Worker (off main thread) and the API section notes 'It all runs in a Web Worker so typing stays smooth.' inp-stays-low=manual.
rubric.notes: Architecture is INP-friendly; an actual INP measurement while typing (debounced re-tag) would confirm.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy work off the main thread / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token-classification inference runs in models/bert-ner/worker.js; the typing handler is debounced.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; API section states worker use

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; avoid fixed widths that overflow narrow viewports.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — verified: no horizontal overflow at 360x740; at-a-glance table + entity chips reflow.

openQuestions: Drive a real WASM run: confirm the live entities on the sample sentences are semantically correct and the confidences are sensible.
Keyboard traversal: is every sample chip + the textarea + any output control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring on both classes?
Are the inline entity-legend swatches (flagged as sub-24px) purely decorative, or do any double as controls that need a 44px target?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for bert-ner: drive every control on mobile+desktop, confirm tap targets/focus/output, then flip support to ok (or unsupported+evidence).
Add a 'streaming redaction' wild demo: mask PER/LOC spans live as the user types, entirely on-device, showing the privacy story.

summary: bert-ner is a strong, honest demo: real token-classification via transformers.js in a Web Worker, accurate at-a-glance disclosure, an honest absent-model download state (no silent ~110 MB transfer), and clean mobile+desktop layout confirmed from screenshots. Remaining work is a driven interaction pass (semantic output check + full keyboard/tap-target verification) before flipping support from needs-review to ok. Revision 2, hand-reviewed from screenshots + runner evidence.
